tldr.text: Google partners with UNICEF and African universities to bring Gemini and NotebookLM to millions of teachers and students worldwide, while Blackstone commits $5B to build a new TPU cloud with Google.
AI legal tech is having a moment — Stilta nabbed $10.5M from a16z and Lexroom closed a $50M Series B, as Mistral quietly snapped up Austrian startup Emmi for its second acquisition in months.

A California jury tossed Elon Musk's breach-of-contract lawsuit against OpenAI in under two hours, handing Sam Altman a clean legal win just as the EU finalizes sweeping AI Act enforcement ahead of August 2026 deadlines.

categories.name: Tools & Products
categories.blurb: Google bets big on AI in education globally, and a new TPU cloud enters the picture.

stories.title: Google + UNICEF bring Gemini to classrooms worldwide
stories.summary: Google announced a three-year partnership with UNICEF to deploy Gemini for Education, NotebookLM, and its ReadAlong reading app across underserved communities globally. The initiative targets both in-school and out-of-school youth, training teachers to use AI for personalized instruction. Separately, Google is expanding access to Gemini and NotebookLM at universities in Ghana, South Africa, and India.

stories.title: Blackstone puts $5B into a Google TPU cloud venture
stories.summary: Blackstone announced a joint venture with Google to stand up a new TPU-powered cloud, with an initial $5 billion equity commitment and a target of 500MW of capacity by 2027. Google will supply the TPUs, software, and services, while Blackstone handles infrastructure. The deal is designed to meet surging demand for AI compute without routing everything through Google Cloud directly.

stories.title: Ettin Reranker family lands six new CrossEncoder models
stories.summary: Hugging Face researcher Tom Aarsen released six new Sentence Transformers CrossEncoder rerankers built on top of ModernBERT encoders, claiming state-of-the-art performance at each size tier. The 1B model processes 928 pairs per second — 2.4x faster than the leading 1.54B competitor — while matching its benchmark score almost exactly. Full training recipes and data are published alongside the models.

categories.name: Startups & Money
categories.blurb: AI legal tech is on a funding tear, and Mistral keeps acquiring its way up the stack.

stories.title: Stilta bags $10.5M to automate patent research
stories.summary: Stilta, an AI platform that automates the research and analytical grunt work behind intellectual property cases, announced a $10.5 million seed round led by Andreessen Horowitz. Y Combinator and operators from OpenAI, Legora, and Lovable also participated. The startup was founded by four people who saw an opening to make patent litigation faster and less expensive.

stories.title: Lexroom raises $50M as investors pile into AI legal tech
stories.summary: Italian legal tech startup Lexroom closed a $50 million Series B led by Left Lane Capital, with Base10 Partners, Eurazeo, and others joining the round. The deal is one of several large checks written into AI-powered legal platforms this month, signaling sustained investor appetite for the sector. Lexroom uses AI to streamline legal research and document workflows for law firms.

stories.title: Mistral acquires Austrian AI startup Emmi
stories.summary: Mistral has struck its second acquisition in a matter of months, picking up Austrian AI startup Emmi to strengthen its capabilities for engineering and manufacturing use cases. Emmi had raised a €15 million seed round last year, and while financial terms were not disclosed, Mistral's CEO described the valuation as 'significant.' The Paris-based company has now raised nearly €2.8 billion and sits at an €11.7 billion valuation.

stories.title: Unframe pulls in $50M Series B for enterprise AI delivery
stories.summary: Unframe, which tailors AI applications to specific enterprise needs, secured $50 million in a Series B round led by Highland Europe. The company was co-founded by Shay Levi and Larissa Schneider and positions itself as a layer between generic AI models and the bespoke requirements of large organizations. The raise underscores continued investor confidence in enterprise AI middleware.

stories.title: Bunch raises $35M to bring AI to VC back-office operations
stories.summary: Bunch, an AI-native platform for private markets operations, closed a $35 million Series B to help venture capital firms manage their operational workflows with AI. The company targets the reporting, compliance, and portfolio-monitoring work that has historically been handled manually at VC funds. The raise reflects growing demand from the investment industry to automate its own internal processes.

categories.name: The Bigger Picture
categories.blurb: Musk loses in court, the EU tightens its AI Act timeline, and regulators everywhere are treating AI agents as a new legal category.

stories.title: Jury tosses Musk's OpenAI lawsuit in under two hours
stories.summary: A jury in Oakland, California ruled against Elon Musk in his breach-of-contract case against OpenAI and its founders, deliberating for less than two hours before finding the lawsuit came too late. The verdict hands Sam Altman a significant legal and reputational win as OpenAI approaches a likely IPO. Musk had alleged that OpenAI violated founding agreements by abandoning its nonprofit mission.

stories.title: EU finalizes AI Act rules with August 2026 deadline looming
stories.summary: The European Commission published updated draft implementation guidance for the AI Act's transparency obligations under Article 50, which take effect August 2, 2026. Systems that interact with people or generate content — including agentic assistants — must carry disclosure measures and machine-readable metadata. Companies face fines up to €35 million or 7% of global revenue for non-compliance with the high-risk system obligations landing in August.

stories.title: Five Eyes agencies warn: don't rush agentic AI into production
stories.summary: Cybersecurity agencies from the Five Eyes alliance published joint guidance titled 'Careful Adoption of Agentic AI Services,' treating autonomous AI systems as new attack surfaces prone to privilege escalation, prompt injection, and opaque decision trails. The guidance explicitly urges organizations in critical infrastructure and government to delay production rollouts until mitigations — including segmented memory and immutable audit logs — are in place. It's the first major joint advisory to treat agentic AI as a distinct security category.

stories.title: China issues first national policy framework for AI agents
stories.summary: China's CAC, NDRC, and MIIT jointly issued 'Implementation Opinions on standardized application and innovative development of intelligent agents' on May 8, defining agent capabilities and setting classification and filing requirements for sensitive sectors. The policy signals Beijing's intent to treat agentic AI as a separate regulated class, with work on technical standards for agent identity and inter-agent protocols underway. It mirrors moves by the EU and Five Eyes to ring-fence autonomous AI systems from existing AI law frameworks.
